comment_text.snp3.9:3.2: Vattasampanno (Sanskrit vṛttasaṁpanna) means “one of good conduct¸one who has fulfilled their duties” rather than having a technical sense of fulfilling religious observances or rites. For example, Manusmṛti 8.179 advises that one should deposit money with someone who is “of good conduct, knows the law, and speaks the truth” (vṛttasampanne dharmajñe satyavādini. See also eg. Mahābhārata 3.188.90b, 13.61.029a, Rāmāyaṇa 1.47.25c).
comment_text.snp3.9:11.1: The Buddha as “eye” evokes the common (eg. Rig Veda 1.164.14, 5.40.8, 5.59.5, 10.10.9) Vedic image of the Sun as the “eye of all” (viśvacakṣāḥ, 7.63.1), the “eye” for “eyes to see” (10.158.4), moving as an unaging wheel through the sky (1.164.14). See DN 16:5.6.3.
comment_text.snp3.9:18.2: I think yāna should be taken literally here as a chariot; consider the classic Indic imagery of flying chariots of the gods.

root_text.snp3.9:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā icchānaṅgale viharati icchānaṅgalavanasaṇḍe.
root_text.snp3.9:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā abhiññātā abhiññātā brāhmaṇamahāsālā icchānaṅgale paṭivasanti, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.snp3.9:1.4: caṅkī brāhmaṇo, tārukkho brāhmaṇo, pokkharasāti brāhmaṇo, jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo, todeyyo brāhmaṇo, aññe ca abhiññātā abhiññātā brāhmaṇamahāsālā.
root_text.snp3.9:1.5: Atha kho vāseṭṭhabhāradvājānaṁ māṇavānaṁ jaṅghāvihāraṁ anucaṅkamantānaṁ anuvicarantānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.snp3.9:2.2: “yato kho, bho, ubhato sujāto hoti mātito ca pitito ca saṁsuddhagahaṇiko yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā akkhitto anupakkuṭṭho jātivādena,
root_text.snp3.9:3.2: “yato kho, bho, sīlavā ca hoti vatasampanno ca,
root_text.snp3.9:3.4: Neva kho asakkhi bhāradvājo māṇavo vāseṭṭhaṁ māṇavaṁ saññāpetuṁ, na pana asakkhi vāseṭṭho māṇavo bhāradvājaṁ māṇavaṁ saññāpetuṁ.
root_text.snp3.9:4.1: Atha kho vāseṭṭho māṇavo bhāradvājaṁ māṇavaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.snp3.9:4.2: “ayaṁ kho, bho bhāradvāja, samaṇo gotamo sakyaputto sakyakulā pabbajito icchānaṅgale viharati icchānaṅgalavanasaṇḍe;
root_text.snp3.9:4.3: taṁ kho pana bhavantaṁ gotamaṁ evaṁ kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggato:
root_text.snp3.9:4.5: Āyāma, bho bhāradvāja, yena samaṇo gotamo tenupasaṅkamissāma; upasaṅkamitvā samaṇaṁ gotamaṁ etamatthaṁ pucchissāma.
root_text.snp3.9:4.6: Yathā no samaṇo gotamo byākarissati tathā naṁ dhāressāmā”ti.
root_text.snp3.9:4.7: “Evaṁ, bho”ti kho bhāradvājo māṇavo vāseṭṭhassa māṇavassa paccassosi.
root_text.snp3.9:5.1: Atha kho vāseṭṭhabhāradvājā māṇavā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.snp3.9:5.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.snp3.9:5.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vāseṭṭho māṇavo bhagavantaṁ gāthāhi ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.snp3.9:69.1: Evaṁ vutte, vāseṭṭhabhāradvājā māṇavā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.snp3.9:69.3: upāsake no bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupete saraṇaṁ gate”ti.

translation_text.snp3.9:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying in a forest near Icchānaṅgala.
translation_text.snp3.9:1.3: Now at that time several very well-known well-to-do brahmins were residing in Icchānaṅgala. They included
translation_text.snp3.9:1.4: the brahmins Caṅkī, Tārukkha, Pokkharasāti, Jānussoṇi, Todeyya, and others.
translation_text.snp3.9:1.5: Then as the students Vāseṭṭha and Bhāradvāja were going for a walk they began to discuss the question:
translation_text.snp3.9:2.2: “When you’re well born on both your mother’s and father’s side, of pure descent, with irrefutable and impeccable genealogy back to the seventh paternal generation—
translation_text.snp3.9:3.2: “When you’re ethical and accomplished in doing your duties—
translation_text.snp3.9:3.4: But neither was able to persuade the other.
translation_text.snp3.9:4.2: “Worthy Bhāradvāja, the ascetic Gotama—a Sakyan, gone forth from a Sakyan family—is staying in a forest near Icchānaṅgala.
translation_text.snp3.9:4.4: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.snp3.9:4.5: Come, let’s go to see him and ask him about this matter.
translation_text.snp3.9:4.6: As he answers, so we’ll remember it.”
translation_text.snp3.9:5.1: So they went to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.snp3.9:5.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side,
translation_text.snp3.9:5.3: and Vāseṭṭha addressed the Buddha in verse:
translation_text.snp3.9:7.2: in all the Vedic experts teach.
translation_text.snp3.9:7.4: we match our tutors in recitation.
translation_text.snp3.9:8.4: one is a brahmin due to birth,
translation_text.snp3.9:8.5: but I declare it’s because of one’s deeds.
translation_text.snp3.9:8.6: Oh Clear-eyed One, know this as our debate.
translation_text.snp3.9:9.1: Since neither of us was able
translation_text.snp3.9:9.3: we’ve come to ask you, sir,
translation_text.snp3.9:10.1: As people honor with joined palms
translation_text.snp3.9:10.2: the moon on the cusp of waxing,
translation_text.snp3.9:11.2: the Eye arisen in the world:
translation_text.snp3.9:11.3: is one a brahmin due to birth,
translation_text.snp3.9:11.5: We don’t know, please tell us,
translation_text.snp3.9:11.6: so we can recognize a brahmin.”
translation_text.snp3.9:14.1: Next there are bugs that crawl or fly,
translation_text.snp3.9:14.2: and so on, to ants and termites.
translation_text.snp3.9:19.1: While the differences between these species
translation_text.snp3.9:20.1: Not by hair nor by head,
translation_text.snp3.9:20.2: not by ear nor by eye,
translation_text.snp3.9:20.3: not by mouth nor by nose,
translation_text.snp3.9:20.4: not by lips nor by eyebrow,
translation_text.snp3.9:21.1: not by shoulder nor by neck,
translation_text.snp3.9:21.2: not by belly nor by back,
translation_text.snp3.9:21.3: not by buttocks nor by breast,
translation_text.snp3.9:21.4: not by groin nor by genitals,
translation_text.snp3.9:22.1: not by hands nor by feet,
translation_text.snp3.9:22.2: not by fingers nor by nails,
translation_text.snp3.9:22.3: not by knees nor by thighs,
translation_text.snp3.9:22.4: not by color nor by voice:
translation_text.snp3.9:22.5: none of these are defined by birth
translation_text.snp3.9:22.6: as it is for other species.
translation_text.snp3.9:24.4: as a farmer, not a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:25.4: as a professional, not a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:26.4: as a trader, not a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:27.4: as a servant, not a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:28.4: as a bandit, not a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:29.4: as a warrior, not a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:30.4: as a sacrificer, not a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:31.4: as a ruler, not a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:32.1: I don’t call someone a brahmin
translation_text.snp3.9:32.2: after the mother’s womb they’re born from.
translation_text.snp3.9:32.6: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:33.3: they’ve slipped their chains and are detached:
translation_text.snp3.9:33.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:34.1: They’ve cut the strap and harness,
translation_text.snp3.9:34.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:35.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:36.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:37.1: Like water from a lotus leaf,
translation_text.snp3.9:37.2: like a mustard seed off a pin-point,
translation_text.snp3.9:37.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:38.2: the end of suffering in this life;
translation_text.snp3.9:38.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:39.2: expert in the path and what is not the path;
translation_text.snp3.9:39.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:40.3: a migrant with no bastion, few in wishes:
translation_text.snp3.9:40.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:41.3: not killing or making others kill:
translation_text.snp3.9:41.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:42.1: Not fighting among those who fight,
translation_text.snp3.9:42.2: quenched among those who have taken up arms,
translation_text.snp3.9:42.3: not grasping among those who grasp:
translation_text.snp3.9:42.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:43.3: like a mustard seed off the point of a pin:
translation_text.snp3.9:43.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:44.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:45.1: They don’t steal anything in the world,
translation_text.snp3.9:45.3: fine or coarse, beautiful or ugly:
translation_text.snp3.9:45.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:46.2: for this world or the next;
translation_text.snp3.9:46.3: with no need for hope, detached:
translation_text.snp3.9:46.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:47.2: knowledge has freed them of indecision,
translation_text.snp3.9:47.3: they’ve arrived at the objective, freedom from death:
translation_text.snp3.9:47.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:48.2: to both good and bad deeds;
translation_text.snp3.9:48.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:49.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:50.1: They’ve got past this grueling swamp
translation_text.snp3.9:50.3: Meditating in stillness, free of indecision,
translation_text.snp3.9:50.4: they have crossed over to the far shore.
translation_text.snp3.9:50.6: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:51.2: and have gone forth from lay life;
translation_text.snp3.9:51.3: they’ve ended rebirth in the sensual realm:
translation_text.snp3.9:51.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:52.2: and have gone forth from lay life;
translation_text.snp3.9:52.3: they’ve ended craving to be reborn:
translation_text.snp3.9:52.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:53.1: They’ve thrown off the human yoke,
translation_text.snp3.9:53.2: and slipped out of the heavenly yoke;
translation_text.snp3.9:53.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:54.2: they’re cooled and free of attachments;
translation_text.snp3.9:54.3: a hero, master of the whole world:
translation_text.snp3.9:54.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:55.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:56.3: the perfected ones with defilements ended:
translation_text.snp3.9:56.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:57.1: They have nothing before or after,
translation_text.snp3.9:57.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:58.1: Captain of the herd, excellent hero,
translation_text.snp3.9:58.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:59.1: They who know their past lives,
translation_text.snp3.9:59.2: see heaven and places of loss,
translation_text.snp3.9:59.3: and have attained the ending of rebirth,
translation_text.snp3.9:59.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.
translation_text.snp3.9:60.1: For name and clan are formulated
translation_text.snp3.9:60.2: as mere convention in the world.
translation_text.snp3.9:61.1: For a long time this misconception
translation_text.snp3.9:61.2: has prejudiced those who don’t understand.
translation_text.snp3.9:61.4: that one is a brahmin by birth.
translation_text.snp3.9:62.1: You’re not a brahmin by birth,
translation_text.snp3.9:62.3: You’re a brahmin by your deeds,
translation_text.snp3.9:63.1: You’re a farmer by your deeds,
translation_text.snp3.9:63.3: you’re a trader by your deeds,
translation_text.snp3.9:63.4: by deeds are you a servant;
translation_text.snp3.9:64.1: you’re a bandit by your deeds,
translation_text.snp3.9:64.3: you’re a sacrificer by your deeds,
translation_text.snp3.9:65.1: In this way the astute regard deeds
translation_text.snp3.9:65.4: they’re expert in deeds and their results.
translation_text.snp3.9:66.1: Deeds make the world go on,
translation_text.snp3.9:66.3: sentient beings are bound by deeds,
translation_text.snp3.9:67.3: that’s how to become a brahmin,
translation_text.snp3.9:68.4: as the Divinity and Sakka to the wise.”
translation_text.snp3.9:69.1: When he had spoken, Vāseṭṭha and Bhāradvāja said to him,
translation_text.snp3.9:69.3: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember us as lay followers who have gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.snp3.9:1.5: anucaṅkamantānaṁ anuvicarantānaṁ → anucaṅkamamānānaṁ anuvicaramānānaṁ (bj, pts-vp-pli1); anucaṅkamānānaṁ anuvicaramānānaṁ (si)
variant_text.snp3.9:4.2: ayaṁ kho, bho → ayaṁ bho (bj, sya-all, mr); ayaṁ kho (pts-vp-pli1)
variant_text.snp3.9:21.4: Na sambādhe na methune → na sambādhā na methunā (sya-all, mr)
